message:
feat(ui): improve detail cache matching for rendering and session line grouping

The detail pane sometimes failed to display loaded conversation data when
search hit payloads were truncated or normalized enough to fail the strict
reload-time identity matcher, even though the cached conversation was correct
for the selected path and source. This was especially visible with shared
database sources (Cursor SQLite) where conversation_id values shift between
reindex runs.

Introduce cached_detail_for_render() which applies a relaxed identity check
suitable for rendering: when the strict matcher fails, it falls back to
matching by source_path + source_id, trusting the already-loaded session view
for display purposes without triggering an unnecessary reload.

Additional improvements:
- Add search_hit_has_secondary_identity_hint() to distinguish hits with
  strong identity signals (line_number, created_at, content, non-ellipsis
  snippet, title) from minimal hits that only carry source_path
- Improve collect_session_hit_lines() to use the new secondary identity hint
  to choose between path-level and message-level grouping, preventing
  unrelated sessions from being grouped together
- Fix conversation_view_matches_hit() to handle stale conversation_id
  gracefully: when a hit carries a conversation_id that doesn't match the
  view but also carries secondary identity hints (content, created_at),
  fall through to field-level matching instead of returning false
- Fix set_detail_scroll_clamped() to skip max_scroll enforcement when
  detail_content_lines is 0, preventing premature clamping before content
  is measured
- Align test helpers (app_with_cached_conversation, make_detail_ready_app)
  so cached_detail conversation fields match the selected hit's identity,
  fixing test flakiness from identity mismatch
- Add clear_test_editor_invocations() helper to prevent editor state leaking
  across tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
